title: देवी अथर्वशीर्षम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देव्यथर्वशीर्षम् ॥

ॐ सर्वे वै देवा देवीमुपतस्थुः कासि त्वं महादेवीति॥१॥

साब्रवीत् — अहं ब्रह्मस्वरूपिणी। मत्तः प्रकृतिपुरुषात्मकं जगत्।
शून्यं चाशून्यं च॥२॥

अहमानन्दानानन्दौ। अहं विज्ञानाविज्ञाने।
अहं ब्रह्माब्रह्मणी वेदितव्ये।
अहं पञ्चभूतान्यपञ्चभूतानि।
अहमखिलं जगत्॥३॥

वेदोऽहमवेदोऽहम्। विद्याहमविद्याहम्।
अजाहमनजाहम्। अधश्चोर्ध्वं च तिर्यक्चाहम्॥४॥

अहं रुद्रेभिर्वसुभिश्चरामि।
अहमादित्यैरुत विश्वदेवैः।
अहं मित्रावरुणावुभौ बिभर्मि।
अहमिन्द्राग्नी अहमश्विनावुभौ॥५॥

अहं सोमं त्वष्टारं पूषणं भगं दधामि।
अहं विष्णुमुरुक्रमं ब्रह्माणमुत प्रजापतिं दधामि॥६॥

अहं दधामि द्रविणं हविष्मते सुप्राव्ये यजमानाय सुन्वते।
अहं राष्ट्री संगमनी वसूनां चिकितुषी प्रथमा यज्ञियानाम्॥७॥

अहं सुवे पितरमस्य मूर्धन् मम योनिरप्स्वन्तः समुद्रे।
य एवं वेद। स दैवीं सम्पदमाप्नोति॥८॥

ते देवा अब्रुवन् — नमो देव्यै महादेव्यै शिवायै सततं नमः।
नमः प्रकृत्यै भद्रायै नियताः प्रणताः स्म ताम्॥९॥

तामग्निवर्णां तपसा ज्वलन्तीं वैरोचनीं कर्मफलेषु जुष्टाम्।
दुर्गां देवीं शरणमहं प्रपद्ये सुतरसि तरसे नमः॥१०॥

देवीं वाचमजनयन्त देवास्तां विश्वरूपाः पशवो वदन्ति।
सा नो मन्द्रेषमूर्जं दुहाना धेनुर्वागस्मानुप सुष्टुतैतु॥११॥

कालरात्रीं ब्रह्मस्तुतां वैष्णवीं स्कन्दमातरम्।
सरस्वतीमदितिं दक्षदुहितरं नमामः पावनां शिवाम्॥१२॥

महालक्ष्म्यै च विद्महे सर्वशक्त्यै च धीमहि।
तन्नो देवी प्रचोदयात्॥१३॥

आदिशक्तिर्महामाया सा परा प्रकृतिस्त्विषा।
तस्याः शिखाया मध्ये तु सूर्यवद्भान्तितेजसे॥१४॥

त्वं स्वाहा त्वं स्वधा त्वं हि वषट्कारः स्वरात्मिका।
सुधा त्वमक्षरे नित्ये त्रिधा मात्रात्मिका स्थिता।
अर्धमात्रा स्थिता नित्या यानुच्चार्या विशेषतः॥१५॥

त्वमेव सन्ध्या सावित्री त्वं देवि जननी परा।
त्वयैतद्धार्यते विश्वं त्वयैतत्सृज्यते जगत्।
त्वयैतत्पाल्यते देवि त्वमत्स्यन्ते च सर्वदा॥१६॥

विसृष्टौ सृष्टिरूपा त्वं स्थितिरूपा च पालने।
तथा संहृतिरूपान्ते जगतोऽस्य जगन्मये॥१७॥

महाविद्या महामाया महामेधा महास्मृतिः।
महामोहा च भवती महादेवी महासुरी॥१८॥

प्रकृतिस्त्वं च सर्वस्य गुणत्रयविभाविनी।
कालरात्रिर्महारात्रिर्मोहरात्रिश्च दारुणा॥१९॥

त्वं श्रीस्त्वमीश्वरी त्वं ह्रीस्त्वं बुद्धिर्बोधलक्षणा।
लज्जा पुष्टिस्तथा तुष्टिस्त्वं शान्तिः क्षान्तिरेव च॥२०॥

खड्गिनी शूलिनी घोरा गदिनी चक्रिणी तथा।
शंखिनी चापिनी बाणभुशुण्डीपरिघायुधा॥२१॥

सौम्या सौम्यतराशेषसौम्येभ्यस्त्वतिसुन्दरी।
परापराणां परमा त्वमेव परमेश्वरी॥२२॥

यच्च किञ्चित्क्वचिद्वस्तु सदसद्वाखिलात्मिके।
तस्य सर्वस्य या शक्तिः सा त्वं किं स्तूयसे तदा॥२३॥

यया त्वया जगत्स्रष्टा जगत्पाताथ यो जगत्।
सोऽपि निद्रावशं नीतः कस्त्वां स्तोतुमिहेश्वरः॥२४॥

सर्वस्य बुद्धिरूपेण जनस्य हृदि संस्थिते।
स्वर्गापवर्गदे देवि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२५॥

कलाकाष्ठादिरूपेण परिणामप्रदायिनि।
विश्वस्योपरतौ शक्ते नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२६॥

सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२७॥

॥ ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः ॥

॥ इति श्री देव्यथर्वशीर्षं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devyatharvashirsham ||

Om Sarve Vai Deva Devim Upatasthuh Kasi Tvam Mahadeviti.||1||

Sabravit — Aham Brahma-svarupini. Mattah Prakriti-purushatmakam Jagat.
Shunyam Cha-shunyam Cha.||2||

Aham Ananda-anandau. Aham Vijnana-avijnane.
Aham Brahma-abrahmani Veditavye.
Aham Pancha-bhutany-apancha-bhutani.
Aham Akhilam Jagat.||3||

Vedo'ham Avedo'ham. Vidya-ham Avidya-ham.
Aja-ham Anaja-ham. Adhash-chordhvam Cha Tiryak Cha-ham.||4||

Aham Rudrebhir Vasubhish Charami.
Aham Adityair Uta Vishva-devaih.
Aham Mitra-varunav Ubhau Bibharmi.
Aham Indragni Aham Ashvinav Ubhau.||5||

Aham Somam Tvashtaram Pushanam Bhagam Dadhami.
Aham Vishnum Urukramam Brahmanam Uta Prajapatim Dadhami.||6||

Aham Dadhami Dravinam Havishmate Supravye Yajamanaya Sunvate.
Aham Rashtri Sangamani Vasunam Chikitushi Prathama Yajniyanam.||7||

Aham Suve Pitaram Asya Murdhan Mama Yonir Apsv-antah Samudre.
Ya Evam Veda. Sa Daivim Sampadam Apnoti.||8||

Te Deva Abruvan — Namo Devyai Mahadevyai Shivayai Satatam Namah.
Namah Prakrityai Bhadrayai Niyatah Pranatah Sma Tam.||9||

Tam Agni-varnam Tapasa Jvalantim Vairochanim Karma-phaleshu Jushtam.
Durgam Devim Sharanam Aham Prapadye Sutarasi Tarase Namah.||10||

Devim Vacham Ajanayanta Devas Tam Vishvarupah Pashavo Vadanti.
Sa No Mandresham Urjam Duhana Dhenur Vag Asman Upa Sushtutaitu.||11||

Kalaratrim Brahma-stutam Vaishnavim Skanda-mataram.
Sarasvatim Aditim Daksha-duhitaram Namamah Pavanam Shivam.||12||

Mahalakshmyai Cha Vidmahe Sarva-shaktyai Cha Dhimahi.
Tan No Devi Prachodayat.||13||

Adi-shaktir Mahamaya Sa Para Prakritis Tvisha.
Tasyah Shikhaya Madhye Tu Surya-vad Bhanti-tejase.||14||

Tvam Svaha Tvam Svadha Tvam Hi Vashatkarah Svaratmika.
Sudha Tvam Akshare Nitye Tridha Matratmika Sthita.
Ardha-matra Sthita Nitya Ya-nucharya Visheshatah.||15||

Tvam Eva Sandhya Savitri Tvam Devi Janani Para.
Tvayaitad Dharyate Vishvam Tvayaitat Srijyate Jagat.
Tvayaitat Palyate Devi Tvam Atsyante Cha Sarvada.||16||

Visrishtau Srishti-rupa Tvam Sthiti-rupa Cha Palane.
Tatha Samhriti-rupante Jagato'sya Jaganmaye.||17||

Mahavidya Mahamaya Mahamedha Mahasmritih.
Mahamoha Cha Bhavati Mahadevi Mahasuri.||18||

Prakritis Tvam Cha Sarvasya Guna-traya-vibhavini.
Kalaratrir Maharatrir Moharatris Cha Daruna.||19||

Tvam Shris Tvam Ishvari Tvam Hris Tvam Buddhir Bodha-lakshana.
Lajja Pushtis Tatha Tushtis Tvam Shantih Kshantir Eva Cha.||20||

Khadgini Shulini Ghora Gadini Chakrini Tatha.
Shankhini Chapini Bana-bhushundi-parighayudha.||21||

Saumya Saumyatara-shesha-saumyebhyas Tvatisundari.
Paraparanam Parama Tvam Eva Parameshvari.||22||

Yat Cha Kinchit Kvachid Vastu Sadasad Vakhilatmike.
Tasya Sarvasya Ya Shaktih Sa Tvam Kim Stuyase Tada.||23||

Yaya Tvaya Jagat Srashta Jagat Patatha Yo Jagat.
So'pi Nidra-vasham Nitah Kas Tvam Stotum Iheshvarah.||24||

Sarvasya Buddhi-rupena Janasya Hridi Samsthite.
Svarga-apavargade Devi Narayani Namo'stu Te.||25||

Kala-kashthadi-rupena Parinama-pradayini.
Vishvasyoparatau Shakte Narayani Namo'stu Te.||26||

Sarva-mangala-mangalye Shive Sarvartha-sadhike.
Sharanye Tryambake Gauri Narayani Namo'stu Te.||27||

|| Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||

|| Iti Shri Devyatharvashirsham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
सभी देवताओं ने देवी के पास जाकर पूछा - हे महादेवी! आप कौन हैं?

उन्होंने कहा - मैं ब्रह्मस्वरूपिणी हूँ। मुझसे प्रकृति-पुरुषात्मक जगत् है, शून्य और अशून्य दोनों मैं ही हूँ। मैं आनन्द और अनानन्द हूँ, विज्ञान और अविज्ञान हूँ। मैं वेद हूँ, अवेद भी हूँ। विद्या हूँ, अविद्या भी हूँ। मैं नीचे, ऊपर और तिर्यक् सब ओर हूँ।

मैं रुद्रों और वसुओं के साथ विचरती हूँ। मैं आदित्यों और विश्वदेवों के साथ हूँ। मैं मित्र-वरुण, इन्द्र-अग्नि और दोनों अश्विनीकुमारों को धारण करती हूँ। मैं विष्णु, ब्रह्मा और प्रजापति को धारण करती हूँ।

देवताओं ने कहा - देवी को, महादेवी को, शिवा को सदा नमस्कार। प्रकृति को, भद्रा को नमस्कार।

हे अग्निवर्णा, तपस्या से ज्वलन्ती, वैरोचनी, कर्मफलों में पूजित दुर्गा देवी! मैं आपकी शरण लेता हूँ।

महालक्ष्मी को हम जानते हैं, सर्वशक्ति का ध्यान करते हैं, वह देवी हमें प्रेरित करें।

आप स्वाहा हैं, स्वधा हैं, वषट्कार हैं। आप ही सन्ध्या, सावित्री और परा जननी हैं। आपसे विश्व धारण होता है, सृष्टि होती है, पालन होता है।

सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके। शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीdeviatharvashirshaमन्त्रस्य अथर्वा (ब्रह्मा) ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् एवं बहुप्रकार छन्दः। श्री भगवती सर्वात्मिका देवता। ह्रीं बीजम्। भुवनेश्वरी शक्तिः। श्री देवीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Supreme Upanishadic text declaring the Goddess as Brahman itself
Invokes complete spiritual knowledge and self-realization
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants liberation (moksha)
Provides protection equivalent to reciting all four Vedas
Invokes the blessing of all auspiciousness and removes all inauspiciousness
Invokes the divine wealth (daivi sampat) mentioned in the text
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Devi Atharvashirsha (देवी अथर्वशीर्षम्) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Atharva Veda (Upanishad), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to supreme Upanishadic text declaring the Goddess as Brahman itself as well as grant the divine wealth (daivi sampat) mentioned in the text. Additionally, it is known to grant complete spiritual knowledge and self-realization. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Dussehra. At 27 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 15 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Atharvashirsha in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Atharvashirsha in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and viniyog. Supreme Upanishadic hymn declaring Goddess as Brahman, from Atharva Veda.
relatedSearches: Aham Brahma Svarupini Devi Upanishad meaning
Sarva Mangala Mangalye Shive verse Devi
Atharva Veda Upanishad Goddess Brahman text
Devi Atharvashirsha vs Durga Saptashati comparison

seoFaqs.question: What is the Devi Atharvashirsha and which Veda does it belong to?
seoFaqs.answer: Devi Atharvashirsha (देवी अथर्वशीर्षम्), also called the Devi Upanishad, is a Shakta Upanishad belonging to the Atharva Veda corpus, traditionally counted among the 108 Upanishads listed in the Muktika Upanishad. The text opens with all the devas approaching the Goddess and asking 'Kasi tvam Mahadeviti' — 'Who are you, O Great Goddess?' — and her sweeping reply 'Aham Brahma-svarupini' — 'I am the very form of Brahman'. In 27 mantras (verses), the Devi reveals herself as identical with the Brahman of the Upanishads, the prakriti-purusha cosmos, the Vedic devas (Rudras, Vasus, Adityas, Indra, Agni, Mitra, Varuna, the Ashvins, Soma, Vishnu and Brahma), Sri-Lakshmi, the Gayatri, the syllables Svaha-Svadha-Vashatkara, Adi Shakti, Mahamaya, the three gunas, the daiva-sampat, and ultimately the Sarva-Mangala-Mangalye Narayani who is the absolute reality. (Sources: Atharva Veda Upanishad corpus; Muktika Upanishad list.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the central message of the Devi Atharvashirsha?
seoFaqs.answer: The central declaration of the text is the Goddess's autobiographical revelation that she is not a sectarian deity but Brahman itself — the same non-dual absolute the Upanishads call Brahman or Atman, here revealed in feminine personal form. Verse 2: 'Aham Brahma-svarupini' (I am Brahman). Verse 3: 'I am ananda and non-ananda, vijnana and avijnana, Brahman and abrahman, the five elements and what is beyond them — I am the entire cosmos'. Verse 4: 'I am Veda and not-Veda, vidya and avidya, the unborn and the not-unborn, what is below, above and across — I am all directions'. Verses 5-8: 'I move with the Rudras and Vasus, I bear Mitra-Varuna, I uphold Vishnu and Brahma'. The Devi is therefore not 'a goddess' but the supreme Brahman whose realisation grants daivi-sampat (divine wealth — Bhagavad Gita 16). (Sources: Devi Atharvashirsha; Devi Bhagavata Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mahalakshmi Gayatri verse in the Devi Atharvashirsha?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 13 of the text contains a powerful Gayatri-format mantra: 'Mahalakshmyai cha Vidmahe, Sarva-shaktyai cha Dhimahi, Tan no Devi Prachodayat' — 'We know Mahalakshmi; we meditate on Sarva-Shakti (the All-Power); may that Devi inspire our intellect'. This is one of the most recited Devi Gayatris in pan-Shakta tradition, used at every Devi archana, in Sri-vidya sadhana, at Lakshmi puja, at Navratri, at Diwali Lakshmi puja, and as the daily japa of householders seeking shri (auspicious wealth — both material and spiritual). It encodes the Shakta insight that Lakshmi (auspiciousness) and Sarva-Shakti (the universal power) are not different — what we call wealth is one face of cosmic shakti, and what we call shakti is not separate from auspiciousness. (Sources: Devi Atharvashirsha verse 13; Lakshmi Tantra.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Sarva-Mangala-Mangalye in this text?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 27 contains the supremely beloved closing mantra: 'Sarva-mangala-mangalye Shive sarvartha-sadhike, Sharanye Tryambake Gauri Narayani namo'stu te' — 'Salutations to you, O Narayani, who are the auspiciousness of all auspiciousness, who are the gracious Shiva, who fulfils all purposes, the refuge, the three-eyed, Gauri'. This same verse occurs at the climax of the Devi Mahatmya (Argala Stotra) and is the most universally recited single Devi-mantra in Hindu tradition. Each name is a teaching: Sarva-mangala-mangalye = all auspiciousness; Shive = the one who is identical with Shiva (the Shakta-Shaiva non-dual reality); Sarvartha-sadhike = grantor of all four purusharthas; Sharanye = the refuge; Tryambake = the three-eyed (one with Tryambaka-Shiva); Gauri = the fair one; Narayani = the universal Narayana-shakti. (Sources: Devi Atharvashirsha verse 27; Devi Mahatmya Argala Stotra.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Devi Atharvashirsha be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Devi Atharvashirsha is recited daily by formal Shakta upasakas, especially at Brahma-muhurta or after the morning sandhya. Its most powerful occasions are: each of the nine nights of Sharad Navratri (Ashvin Shukla Pratipada to Navami) — recited 1, 3, 9, 11 or 108 times during these nine days; Vasanta Navratri (Chaitra Shukla Pratipada to Navami); each Krishna Paksha Ashtami; every Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) — the two weekdays sacred to the Goddess; Durgashtami (the Ashtami of Sharad Navratri); and Diwali night for Mahalakshmi. The phalashruti enjoins that recitation at the four sandhyas (sunrise, midday, sunset and midnight) by a Shakta sadhaka grants the merit of all Vedas. Shri-vidya upasakas chant it as part of the Lalita-saparya as well. (Sources: Devi Bhagavata; classical Shakta paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Devi Atharvashirsha and Durga Saptashati?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are core Shakta texts but serve different functions. Durga Saptashati (also called Devi Mahatmya or Chandi Path), drawn from the Markandeya Purana, is a 700-verse narrative parayana text with three carita (stories) of the Goddess slaying Madhu-Kaitabha, Mahishasura and Shumbha-Nishumbha — chanted ritually with Argala, Kilaka, Kavacha, Navarna mantra and prescribed mala-bandha. Devi Atharvashirsha, by contrast, is an Upanishadic text of 27 mantras that does not narrate stories — it is a direct philosophical revelation by the Devi of her identity with Brahman, blending Vedanta, Pancharatra, Shakta and Smarta theologies. Saptashati is for parayana, propitiation and battle-against-asuras (inner and outer). Atharvashirsha is for jnana-upasana, self-realisation through Shakta-Vedanta and as nityapaath. They are best practised together. (Sources: Markandeya Purana; classical Shakta paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Devi Atharvashirsha confer?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti woven through the text enumerates remarkable benefits: (a) reciting it grants the merit equivalent to studying all four Vedas (because the Devi is the Veda herself — verse 4); (b) protects from all great fears (sarva-mahabhaya); (c) destroys all sins (sarva-papa-prashamana); (d) bestows daivi-sampat — the divine wealth of the Bhagavad Gita 16 (fearlessness, purity, charity, austerity, study, simplicity, non-violence, truth, etc.); (e) grants the four purusharthas (dharma, artha, kama, moksha); (f) brings the grace of all the deities named in the text; (g) ensures protection during pregnancy, childbirth, illness and crisis; (h) used in Navratri parayana, brings the merit of completing a full Chandi-yajna; (i) for the jnani, grants direct realisation of the non-dual Brahman in feminine form. (Sources: Devi Atharvashirsha phalashruti; Devi Bhagavata.)
